title: pilosella fusca subsp. atropurpurea
intro: Pilosella fusca subsp. atropurpurea, also known as purple mouse-ear, is a subspecies of the Pilosella fusca plant, which belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. The medicinal properties of Pilosella fusca subsp. atropurpurea are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal activities. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin issues.
